headlines.headline: Gold Falls as Hawkish Fed Outweighs U.S.-Iran Deal Relief
headlines.description: Gold fell in early Asian trade. The precious metal’s price is being weighed down by a more hawkish Fed outweighing relief from the U.S.-Iran interim peace deal, ANZ said.

headlines.headline: Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Hike Bets
headlines.description:
Traders piled into betting on interest-rate hikes as soon as next month after Kevin Warsh used his debut press conference as Federal Reserve chairman to make clear the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation.

headlines.description: Opening a gold IRA removes the pressure of investing in physical gold. You can diversify your portfolio with precious metals through these gold IRAs.

headlines.headline: Gold And Silver Futures Fall As Fed Holds Rates Steady, Indicates Future Hikes
headlines.description: Gold and silver futures both declined Thursday morning, one day after the Federal Reserve held rates steady and suggested future hikes are coming.

headlines.headline: BOE’s Bailey Appeals for Help on X Posts Showing Fake Farage Fight
headlines.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey appealed for help in identifying who is behind a barrage of bizarre social media posts using fake images of him fighting Reform UK leader Nigel Farage.

headlines.headline: Treasury yields mixed as investors digest Warsh's first Fed meet
headlines.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — was largely flat at 4.457%.

headlines.headline: Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ship Back for First Time Since War Began
headlines.description: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran …

headlines.headline: PBOC’s New Rate Regime to Aid Liquidity and Bonds, Analysts Say
headlines.description: The People’s Bank of China’s latest refinement of its monetary policy toolkit is expected to damp money-market volatility and support bonds, according to analysts.

headlines.headline: Oil falls as International Energy Agency forecasts supply glut next year after U.S.-Iran deal
headlines.description: Oil declines as Mideast peace prospects rise following U.S.-Iran deal.

headlines.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Warsh's Fed debut and Iran-U.S. deal in focus
headlines.description: Kevin Warsh chaired his first Fed meeting, sending stocks lower while Trump reportedly signed the deal with Iran.

headlines.headline: Stock market today: Live updates
headlines.description: After hitting a fresh intraday all-time high, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ultimately closed more than 500 points lower on Wednesday.

headlines.headline: Analysis: Chairman Kevin Warsh’s task forces are the key to understanding the new Fed
headlines.description: The new leader of the Fed left interest rates alone but made clear how he plans to rewire the central bank.

headlines.headline: Warsh experiences worst 'Fed day' S&P 500 performance for a new chair since 1994
headlines.description: The S&P 500 tumbled more than 1% in Wednesday's session, with losses steepening during Warsh' innagural press conference as chair.

headlines.headline: Jeffrey Gundlach says Fed's Warsh is not going to be the 'easy money' chairman many hoped for
headlines.description: Gundlach said Warsh's stance reduces the risk of overly accommodative monetary policy that could reignite inflation and push longer-term borrowing costs higher.

headlines.headline: Fed holds rates steady, officials split over possible hikes this year
headlines.description: The meeting, chaired for the first time by Kevin Warsh, signalled a shift in the Fed’s communication strategy as it grapples with rising inflation pressures from energy costs and geopolitical tensions

headlines.headline: Fed projections call for a rate hike in 2026, but Chairman Warsh abstained
headlines.description: The median projection called for the federal funds rate to end 2026 at 3.8%, a quarter percentage point above the current target range.

headlines.description: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released its interest rate decision.

headlines.headline: Copper Miner Antofagasta Seeks to Link Ore Sales to Spot Market
headlines.description: Chile’s Antofagasta Plc has approached Chinese copper smelters with a proposal to price contractual sales of copper ore using spot-market indexes, a fresh sign that a decades-old system based on fixed-price deals is under strain.

headlines.headline: Iraq Takes Steps to Boost Oil Exports When Hormuz Reopens
headlines.description: Iraq is preparing to boost oil exports from its southern ports with a formal deal to open the Strait of Hormuz scheduled to be signed on Friday.

headlines.headline: ECB’s Lagarde Warns AI Is Huge Risk for Financial Stability
headlines.description: Artificial intelligence can potentially lead to dangerous financial crises, and the European Central Bank is determined to ensure that doesn’t happen, President Christine Lagarde said.

headlines.headline: From supply shock to oil glut: IEA flags scale of demand destruction caused by Iran war
headlines.description: The energy supply shock triggered by the Iran War has prompted the IEA to slash its global oil demand forecast.

headlines.headline: Oil prices continue slide amid hopes for peace, opening of Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Brent crude drops to lowest price since early March, before signing of framework deal to end US-Israel war on Iran.

headlines.headline: The City of London's vanishing analysts
headlines.description: The annual Extel survey shines a light on the fortunes of equity analysts covering the U.K. stock market.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan hikes rates to highest since 1995 as yen languishes at historic lows
headlines.description: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.

headlines.headline: Lagarde Says ECB Has Started to See Second Round Effects
headlines.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said high energy prices are starting to feed through to other parts of the economy, according to an interview with France Culture.

headlines.headline: BOJ Set to Hike Rates to Highest Since 1995 Despite Ueda Absence
headlines.description: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise its benchmark interest rate to the highest level since 1995 at the first regular policy meeting ever held without the governor in attendance.

headlines.headline: Bailey Defends BOE Gilt Sales After Criticism From Farage
headlines.description: Governor Andrew Bailey defended the Bank of England’s buying and selling of UK government bonds following criticism of the policy, including from Nigel Farage’s populist Reform UK party.

headlines.headline: Week Ahead: 7 G10 Central Banks Meet, BOJ To Hike, Warsh Chairs First FOMC, G7 Summit
headlines.description: After threatening a new round of intense bombing of Iran, the US relented and suggested an agreement was near. Read more here.

headlines.headline: ECB's Lagarde Warns of Impact of Prolonged Energy Shock
headlines.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde says euro-zone inflation should return to the 2% target by the second half of 2027, warning that a prolonged war in Iran, and subsequent impact on energy prices, would be a drag on growth. She made the comments at a news conference in Frankfurt after...

headlines.headline: ECB hikes interest rates for first time since 2023 as Iran war ramps-up energy costs
BOE’s Bailey Warns of Possible AI Rationing on Capacity Limits
headlines.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey suggested that artificial intelligence may need to be rationed because a lack of energy capacity will restrain the ability for every economic sector to deploy the new technology.
